t_ch: 你居然会用「毕业」这种词语？
t_en: You know the meaning of the word "graduate"?

t_ch: 哼，我尤利安会的词语可多了，你也多看点儿《冒险鼹鼠队》吧！
t_en: Hmph, I know a lot of words. You should read more of The Adventurous Moles!

t_ch: 总之，要是有一天还能买到新的《冒险鼹鼠队》的话，我还会去看看的。
t_en: Anyway, if I can buy new editions of The Adventurous Moles one day, I'll read it.

t_ch: …我做得到吗？
t_en: ... Can I even manage to do that, I wonder?

t_ch: 不对，只是作者偷懒了。
t_en: No, the author is just being lazy.

t_ch: 原来如此，看来是我给大人找借口了。
t_en: I see. It seems that I just made an excuse for adults.

t_ch: 你不想看看整本书的故事吗？
t_en: Don't you want to read the whole book?

t_ch: …想看是想看的。
t_en: ...I do.

t_ch: 但我在想自己是不是应该从《冒险鼹鼠队》毕业了，毕竟我也长大了嘛。{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，你是怎么想的呢？
t_en: But I wonder if I should graduate from The Adventurous Moles. I've grown up after all. What do you think?

t_ch: （应该不是这里，这里什么也挡不住。）
t_en: (It shouldn't be here. This can't block anything.)

t_ch: （应该不是这里，到别处再找找看吧。）
t_en: (It shouldn't be here. Look elsewhere.)

t_ch: （应该不是这里，这里和周围也没有什么区别。）
t_en: (It shouldn't be here. It's no different from anywhere else.)

t_ch: （三把兵器便是一座岛屿，重兵器好像也是兵器，但是直觉告诉我并不是这里…）
t_en: (Three weapons make an island. Heavy weapons are weapons too, but my intuition tells me it's not here...)

t_ch: （既然在这里捡到了缺失的书页，那么谜题就在前面咖啡馆里吧…）
t_en: (Since I found the missing page here, the riddle should be in the café up ahead...)

t_ch: （露天咖啡馆里，只有一处的椅子与众不同…在哪里呢？）
t_en: (In the open-air café, there is only one chair that stands out... Where is it?)

t_ch: （能藏住一个人的身影…难道说那一处椅子所在位置比较高？）
t_en: (It can hide a person... Is it talking about the height of the chair?)

t_ch: （原来是在这里，看来是这把椅子挡住了尾随者。）
t_en: (So here it is. This chair seems to have blocked the person from view.)

t_ch: （既然在这里捡到了缺失的书页，那么跟矿灯有关的谜题就在前面吧…）
t_en: (Since I found the missing page here, then the riddle about the Miner's Lamp should be up ahead...)

t_ch: （「让一切都回到它本来的位置」…这是指矿灯不在它原本的位置吗？）
t_en: ("Let everything return to its original position"... Does this mean that the miner's lamp is not in its original position?)

t_ch: （铁丝网上的矿灯好像空缺了一处……）
t_en: (There seems to be a miner's lamp missing on the barbed wire...)

t_ch: （原来这就是「让群星回到天空」啊。）
t_en: (So this is what it meant by "let the stars return to the sky.")

t_ch: （既然在这里捡到了缺失的书页，那么跟货箱、货架有关的谜题就在前面吧…）
t_en: (Since the missing pages were found here, the riddles about the crates and shelves should be up ahead...)

t_ch: （「货架，货箱，摆放，相同。」…这是什么意思呢？）
t_en: ("Shelves, crates, arrange, same"... What does that mean?)

t_ch: （两边的货架上的货箱好像是可以移动的……）
t_en: (The crates on the shelves on both sides seem to be movable...)

t_ch: （鼹鼠队的小伙伴们就是这样找到了暗门吧……）
t_en: (That's how the Mole team found the secret door...)

t_ch: （既然在这里捡到了缺失的书页，那么跟战壕、兵器有关的谜题就在前面吧…）
t_en: (Since I found the missing pages here, the riddles about the trenches and weapons should be up ahead...)

t_ch: （「三把兵器便是一座岛屿」…这岛屿可真够随便的。）
t_en: ("Three weapons make an island"... What a lazy metaphor.)

t_ch: （「宝藏放在四座岛屿的交汇之处」…交汇之处又在哪里呢？）
t_en: ("The treasure is at the intersection of the four islands"... Where is the intersection?)

t_ch: （原来如此，小鼹鼠们就这样找到了德雷克的宝藏。）
t_en: (I see. So this is how the Mole Team found Drake's treasure.)

t_ch: 一台「熊蜂」型的自动机兵瘫痪在路边——若不出意外，它可引领你找到些有趣的东西。
t_en: An automaton of the Bumblebee model is lying paralyzed on the roadside. If there are no surprises, it might lead you to something interesting.

t_ch: （或许可以看看它有无修复的可能……）
t_en: (Perhaps I should check whether it can be repaired...)

t_ch: 请请请请根据面板提示进行操作作作，确认探测路径径径径——
t_en: P—p—p—please follow the instructions on the p—p—p—panel and confirm the exploration p—p—p—path.

t_ch: （查看一下它的操作面板吧……）
t_en: (Okay, let me take a look at its control panel...)

t_ch: 在路径前方侦测到敌敌敌敌意反应——
t_en: Hostile enemy t—t—t—targets detected ahead...

t_ch: 在在在在周围地面侦测到疑似爆炸物反应，请请请在《银鬃铁卫爆炸物威胁搜查排除手册册册》指导下将其排除除除除——
t_en: Suspected exp—p—p—plosive is detected in the vicinity. P—p—p—please follow the instructions from the "Silvermane Guards' Guide to Exp—p—p—plosive Disposal" to deactivate the b—b—b—bomb.

t_ch: （它似乎在附近发现了什么……）
t_en: (It seems to have detected something near...)

t_ch: （应该不可能是爆炸物吧……）
t_en: (Surely, it can't be an actual bomb, right?)

t_ch: （自动机兵自顾自地飞走了……）
t_en: (The automaton flew away on its own...)

t_ch: 侦侦侦测到路径中存在障碍碍碍碍，请求排除除除除——
t_en: De—De—De—Detecting obstacles in th—th—the way. Please cl—cl—clear the path—

t_ch: （似乎挡住了自动机兵的前进路线…为它让开道路吧。）
t_en: (Seems like something's blocking the automaton's path... Make way for it.)

t_ch: 我听说你有事儿找我。
t_en: I heard you are looking for me.

t_ch: 我想想…哦，对了，确实有件事想拜托你。
t_en: Let's see... oh, yes, there is indeed something that could use your help.

t_ch: 有位年事已高的矿车工师傅打算退休了，但是吧…他和他现在的徒弟实在不对付。估计你也知道我想说什么了——徒弟不出师，师傅也难隐退嘛。
t_en: An elderly mine cart operator intends to retire, but... he's not really hitting it off with his current apprentice. I guess you already know what I'm getting at. If the apprentice can't complete his training, then the master won't be able to retire in peace.

t_ch: 所以，我想请你去当那位笨徒弟的…呃，助教。
t_en: So, I'd like to ask you to be... um... assistant instructor for that not-so-bright apprentice.

t_ch: 助教？
t_en: Assistant instructor?

t_ch: 不是，我也不懂矿车啊。
t_en: Uh, no. I know nothing of mining carts.

t_ch: 我给你推荐个好人选，他叫桑博…
t_en: I know just the man for this job. His name is Sampo...

t_ch: 哈？他可不行。你这是给我找麻烦啊。
t_en: Huh? No way. He'd bring nothing but trouble.

t_ch: 你放心，说是让你做助教，其实不需要什么专业知识。
t_en: Relax. Though the title is "assistant instructor," it doesn't actually require much technical know-how.

t_ch: 那老头子能力很强，可惜脾气不好，倔得很。遇到悟性高的徒弟还好，但是现在不是没遇到嘛…就只能请你想办法帮帮他了。
t_en: That senior mine cart operator is very capable, but he has a bad temper and can be very stubborn. Things would be good if he had a gifted apprentice to begin with, but that's sadly not the case right now... So please find a way to help him.

t_ch: 怎么样，能帮我这个忙吗？
t_en: What say you? Can you do me this favor?

t_ch: 没问题，包在我身上。
t_en: Not a problem. Leave it to me.

t_ch: 我尽量吧…
t_en: I suppose I can give it a try...

t_ch: 他们平时就待在矿区的流浪者避难所。我之前跟老师傅打过招呼了，见了他只要报我的名字就行。
t_en: They usually stay at the vagrant shelter in the mines. I've already told that senior mine cart operator about this. When you see him, just say my name, and he'll know.

t_ch: 看好了啊，这玩意叫转盘控制器。你把手放在这儿，再这样一下……
t_en: Watch carefully. This thing is called the turntable controller. You put your hand here, and then do this...

t_ch: …你是打哪儿来的？咋，找我有事？
t_en: ...Just where did you pop out from? Do you need me for something?

t_ch: 我奉命来担任你的助教。
t_en: I'm sent here to be your assistant instructor.

t_ch: 啥？什么助教……
t_en: Huh? What assistant instructor...

t_ch: 嚯…我知道了，安东尼娜让你来的吧？
t_en: Oh... I see. Antonia sent you here, didn't she?

t_ch: 矿头安东尼娜叫我来的。
t_en: Antonia asked me to come.

t_ch: 啥？安东尼娜让你来的？
t_en: What? Antonia sent you here?

t_ch: 嚯…我知道了，你就是那个什么「助教」是吧？
t_en: Oh... I see. You must be that "assistant instructor," right?

t_ch: 唉，那姑娘老是自作主张…跟她说了多少次了，这点小事我搞得定……
t_en: *sigh* That girl always makes decisions for me without first consulting me. I've told her many times that I can deal with this myself...

t_ch: 不过你来都来了，我也不能让你闲着。
t_en: But now that you're here, I can't let you just hang around and do nothing.

t_ch: 现在就开始吧。
t_en: Let's get started.

t_ch: 行，来搭把手——帮我先把教学场地布置一下。
t_en: Alright, come give me a hand. Help me set things up.

t_ch: 要不还是让我闲着吧…
t_en: I am happy to just hang around.

t_ch: 哼，少废话，矿山里的规矩就是少说多做。你来都来了，做完我就让你休息。
t_en: Ha, good one. We have a rule in the mines — less talking and more working. I'll however let you take a break after the work's done.

t_ch: 来，搭把手，帮我先把教学场地布置一下。
t_en: Come on, give me a hand. Help me set things up.

t_ch: 这是一辆快要报废的矿车。出于安全考虑，它现在只能作为教具在矿场里继续发光发热了。
t_en: This is a mining cart that's about to be scrapped. For safety reasons, it can only continue to serve its purpose in the mines as a teaching prop.

t_ch: 你捡到了一台轨道控制器。复杂的机械结构被铁皮封得严严实实，只留下了一根操作杆，被人拉下后可以自动复位。
t_en: You picked up a Track Controller. The complex mechanical structure is sealed tightly within the iron covering, leaving only a lever for operation. The lever automatically returns to the default position after being pulled down.

t_ch: 有经验的矿车工可以通过合理布线将控制器与轨道相连，从而使用操作杆调整轨道方向。
t_en: Experienced mine cart operators can adjust the track direction using the operating lever, after connecting the controller to the track through an appropriate wiring arrangement.

t_ch: 你找到了矿车用阻车器。它可以经由巧妙设计的机械结构及时将矿车车轮抱死，防止矿车冲出轨道。
t_en: You've found the Mine Cart Blocker. Its clever design enables it to prevent any cart from running off the track by clamping its wheels almost immediately.

t_ch: 这么快就回来了，我的工具你准备齐了？
t_en: That was quick. Have you already managed to get all the teaching props ready?

t_ch: （提交教具）
t_en: (Submit Teaching Props)

t_ch: 辛苦你了，该上实践课了，我这就准备场地…你也收拾停当后就来找我吧。
t_en: Thanks. It's almost time for a demonstration. I'm going to prepare the demo site... Come find me after you've tidied things up.

t_ch: 全部准备好了，再来找我吧。
t_en: Come find me when everything is ready.

t_ch: 还得等等。
t_en: Still need a bit more time.

t_ch: 没关系，我这蠢徒弟什么都还没搞明白，你先去忙吧。
t_en: That's alright. My useless apprentice still hasn't mastered anything I've taught him so far. You can carry on with what you were doing.

t_ch: 好了…小子，站过来！看见这片场地了吗？
t_en: All ready... Hey kid, come over here! Do you see what's in front of you?

t_ch: 矿车和铁轨就不说了，你打小就长在下层区，哪怕不认识也该听说过。
t_en: I'm not going to explain how mine carts and tracks work. You grew up in the Underworld, so even if you've never seen them before, you've probably heard of them.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 重点看这个——「轨道控制器」。
t_en: The thing you need to pay attention to is this — the track controller.

t_ch: 道理很简单，像我刚才说的那样操作控制器，道岔上原本并不相连的轨道就接在一起了。
t_en: The principle is very simple. Operate the controller like I just told you, and the disconnected tracks on the turnout will then be connected together.

t_ch: 之后呢，已经连通的轨道上的地髓灯就会亮起来。你可以根据这个来判断矿车会往哪个方向走。
t_en: After that, the Geomarrow light on the connected track will light up. You can then use this to judge which direction the mine cart will go.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Hmm?

t_ch: 然后是这个「阻车器」。顾名思义，阻车器会堵住矿车前进的路线。要是矿车被阻车器拦住，咱就只能让它沿着轨道倒回起点了。
t_en: Then comes this blocker. As the name implies, the blocker will block the route of the mine cart. If the mine cart is stopped by one of these, then the only thing we can do is send it back to where it just came from.
Hmm...

t_ch: 咱要做的就是通过规划轨道、通过控制器调整轨道方向，将矿车推到原定的目的地。
t_en: All we need to do is plan out the path and adjust the track direction through the controller to push the mining cart to its destination.

t_ch: 就这些，我讲完了。你刚才哼哼唧唧没蹦出半句话，也不知道到底学会没……
t_en: That's it, I'm done. You just groaned and moaned and didn't say anything, so I'm not sure if you're getting all of this...

t_ch: 不过我有的是办法测你。这不，场地已经给你搭好了，你上手实操给我看！
t_en: But, I have a way to test you. See, the test rig has been set up for you already. Show me how you'll go about operating it!

t_ch: 这…我感觉不太行。
t_en: Uh... I'm not sure about this.

t_ch: 咋就不行了？
t_en: What do you mean by that?

t_ch: 就…太难了呗。
t_en: Well... it's way too hard.

t_ch: …再说一次？
t_en: ...What did you say?

t_ch: 太难了呗，这咋操作嘛…看不太懂。
t_en: Like I said, it's too hard. How should I go about it... I don't understand.

t_ch: 我这都教了你多少次了，啊？平时讲起理论的时候头头是道，该你上手操作的时候就给我这畏手畏脚？
t_en: How many times have I taught you this already, huh? When we talk about the theory, you seem to be pretty onto it, but why's it always a cop-out when it comes to the real thing?

t_ch: 但凡有点脑子的都早该学会了！操作控制器、换轨道、前进、再操作控制器、然后倒车。这有什么难的？
t_en: Anyone with any shred of intelligence should have mastered this a long time ago! Operate the controller, change the track, move forward, operate the controller again, and then reverse. Where's the difficulty in that?

t_ch: …还是看不懂，嘿嘿。
t_en: ...I still can't figure it out, hehe.

t_ch: …我看你这小子就是想把我气死！
t_en: ... Brat, you're trying to send me to an early grave, aren't you!?

t_ch: 你，过来！给他演示一遍，看看是不是有点脑子就能学会！
t_en: You, come here! Show him how it's done. Anyone with half a brain can do it!

t_ch: 我只是个助教…
t_en: I'm only an instructor assistant...

t_ch: 少废话，安东尼娜叫你来，不就是为了这个？
t_en: Enough nonsense. Isn't this why Antonia sent you here in the first place?

t_ch: 看不懂，嘿嘿。
t_en: I don't get it either. Hehe.

t_ch: 别装蒜，我给他讲解的时候你明明就在边上，看也该看会了！
t_en: Stop playing dumb. You were there when I was explaining everything. You should know just by watching!

t_ch: 那就开始吧。如果你需要重新布置场地的话，随时和我说。
t_en: Then let's get started. If you want the mine carts to be reset, feel free to talk to me.

t_ch: 还真解决了……
t_en: You actually solved it...

t_ch: 看到没？我早说过，但凡有点脑子的都早该学会了！
t_en: See what I mean? I told you anyone with half a brain should've learned all of this ages ago!

t_ch: 这助教当得可真辛苦…
t_en: Being an assistant instructor isn't easy...

t_ch: 这话听着怎么不太对呢…
t_en: Hey, whom are you calling half a brain...

t_ch: 区区矿车…看来我也要拿出真本事了……
t_en: It's just a mine cart... I guess it's time to get serious...

t_ch: 嘁，臭小子…刚才怎么没见你这么有干劲？我看你平时就是抽懒筋……
t_en: Tsk... So now you are getting motivated? Were you just slacking off most of the time?

t_ch: 回头再收拾我这懒徒弟，今天先多谢你帮忙了。
t_en: I'll deal with my lazy apprentice later. Thank you for the help today.

t_ch: 这些，拿着吧，就当是我的谢礼了。哦对，也帮我跟安东尼娜打声招呼。
t_en: Here, take this as your reward. Oh yeah, and remember to say hello to Antonia for me.

t_ch: 不过啊，今天给这臭小子出的题只能说是开胃菜——真正难的还在后头呢。你要是对这矿车感兴趣，我现在就能给你布置场地。
t_en: That being said, the puzzle that I gave to the brat today was nothing more than an appetizer. The tricky stuff has yet to come. If you're interested in mine carts, I can set up the site for you.

t_ch: 要是有你这种机灵的{M#小伙子}{F#小姑娘}在旁边刺激刺激这臭小子，他估计就能上点心咯。
t_en: With a smart {M#boy}{F#girl} like you to motivate him, that brat might actually put a bit more effort into things.

t_ch: 怎么，你还真对这矿车感兴趣？那成，场地已经铺好了，这回的难度可是实打实的！要不你也来一起看看？
t_en: I guess you really are interested in mine carts? Well, I've laid out the site for you already. I've made sure it's challenging this time! Why don't you give it a try?

t_ch: 看我的！
t_en: Watch this!

t_ch: 年轻人就是得有冲劲！臭小子，给我好好学着点！
t_en: Youngsters should be motivated! You could learn a thing or two kid!

t_ch: 不就是个矿车嘛，看我——哎呦，师傅别掐了别掐了疼疼疼！
t_en: Seems easy enough, just watch me — Ouch! Master, stop pinching me, it hurts!

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那等你有空再来。
t_en: That's fine. Come back when you have time.

t_ch: 嚯，你这{M#小子}{F#小姑娘}不简单啊！能搞定这个问题的话，基本可以在矿场里负责些简单的调度工作了。
t_en: Oho, nicely done {M#lad}{F#young lady}! If you can handle this, you can take care of some simple coordinating duties in the mine.

t_ch: 不错，你可是给这臭小子做榜样了！明天记得再来。在那之前，我得先想想再出点什么题……
t_en: Not bad. You're setting an example for the brat! Remember to come back tomorrow. I have to think of some more tests first...

t_ch: 怎么，要重新布置场地吗？
t_en: So, do you want me to reset the mine carts for you?

t_ch: 请帮我重新布置场地。
t_en: Yes. Please help me reset the carts.

t_ch: 好嘞，你等一下……
t_en: Okay, just one moment...

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: There's no need.

t_ch: 嗯？你来得挺早嘛。不过我还在想新的谜题，你之后再过来看看吧。
t_en: Hmm? You're early. I'm still working on a new puzzle, so you'll have to come back later.

t_ch: 我有些问题想问…
t_en: I still have some more questions...

t_ch: 你怎么收到这个徒弟的？
t_en: How did you meet your current apprentice?

t_ch: 嗯？你问这小子啊…这说来话长了，这小子可怜得很哪。
t_en: Hmm? You mean the kid... Well, that's a long story. He's had a pretty tough life so far.

t_ch: 他爹本来和我一起在矿里做工，后来跟那群流浪者去裂界里拾荒碰运气…结果人再没回来过。
t_en: His father was working with me in the mines before. But he decided to try his luck scavenging in the Fragmentum with a group of vagrants... He never came back.

t_ch: 我看他无依无靠的，就想着把我这铺铁轨的手艺传给他。他也好靠自己讨个饭吃。
t_en: I saw he had no one to rely on, so I thought to pass on my track-laying skills to him. With those skills, he can make a living for himself.

t_ch: 你在这片矿区做了很久吗？
t_en: Have you been working in these mines for a long time?

t_ch: 那可不！我跟着这矿队走南闯北好多年咯。这片矿区开辟出来也就不到小二十年的事，不过具体啥时候发现的我是记不得了。
t_en: That's right! I've been with this mining team for many years. I can't remember exactly how long, but this mine has been operating for nearly twenty years now.

t_ch: 你看到矿区中央那片大矿脉没？壮观吧！之前做勘测的蠢货怎么就没早点发现，没想到最值钱的玩意居然灯下黑啊……
t_en: Do you see that big vein in the middle of the mine? Absolutely stunning! Why didn't those useless surveyors find it earlier! To think that the most valuable prize was hiding in plain sight...

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 没什么事了。
t_en: It's nothing.

t_ch: …哦，是你啊。师傅还在准备新题目呢，我稍微偷会儿懒……
t_en: ... Oh, it's you. My master is still preparing his new puzzle, so I'm taking the chance to relax for a while...

t_ch: 我有些问题想问…
t_en: I still have some more questions...

t_ch: 你为什么会来做矿车工？
t_en: What drove you to become a mine cart operator?

t_ch: 嗯？怎么想起来问这个？
t_en: Hmm? Why the sudden interest?

t_ch: 我爹妈都走得早嘛。生活所迫，加上师傅以前是我爹的朋友，我就被那老头子收养咯。
t_en: Well, I guess, life happens. You see, my parents... they died a long time ago. My master, who was a friend of my father, adopted me.

t_ch: 老头对我也挺好的。我知道他怕我没个傍身的手艺，将来在这贝洛伯格吃不开，这才把调度矿车的活计传给我了。
t_en: The old man has been very good to me. I know he's worried that I wouldn't be able to make a good living in Belobog without the right skills. I guess that's why he decided to pass his skills to me.

t_ch: 可我就是忍不住偷懒…这也没什么吧？我还年轻，有的是时间……
t_en: But I just can't help being lazy... I mean, that's okay, right? I'm still young, so I still have plenty of time...

t_ch: 矿车工平时都负责些什么？
t_en: What does a mine cart operator usually do?

t_ch: 师傅说，矿车工是矿山的血液，是咱保证了地髓能够源源不断从矿道深处运输出来。
t_en: My master said that the mine cart operator is the lifeblood of the mine. The operator ensures the Geomarrow can be continuously transported out of the depths of the mine.

t_ch: 只有通过我们铺设的轨道，地髓才能被送达它应该前往的地点。
t_en: Geomarrow can only be delivered to the place it's supposed to go through the tracks we lay.

t_ch: 听着很光鲜，但…其实我还是想干点大事。我可不想一辈子被圈在这矿区里。
t_en: I mean it sounds pretty important... But still I want to do something much bigger in life. I don't want to be trapped in these mines forever.

t_ch: 算了，想这么多也没用…我暂时留在这儿，就当是为了报答师傅的养育之恩吧，这样我心里多少舒服点。
t_en: Forget it. I guess there's no point thinking so far ahead... I have to stay here for the time being to repay the debt of kindness I owe to my master. If I think like this, I feel more at ease.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 没什么事了。
t_en: It's nothing.

t_ch: 看好了啊，{M#小子}{F#小姑娘}。那个亮着<color=#dbc291ff>黄灯</color>的阻车器，就是<color=#dbc291ff>满载矿车</color>的泊位。
t_en: Watch carefully, {M#boy}{F#girl}. The blocker with the <color=#dbc291ff>yellow light</color> on is the stop for <color=#dbc291ff>the loaded mine carts</color>.

t_ch: 而<color=#dbc291ff>空载矿车</color>则应该开往亮着<color=#dbc291ff>蓝灯</color>的停泊处。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>empty mine cart</color> should be driven to the stop with <color=#dbc291ff>blue lights</color>.

t_ch: 如果矿车需要<color=#dbc291ff>装卸地髓</color>的话，就得在<color=#dbc291ff>地髓矿堆旁的泊位</color>进行装卸了。
t_en: If a mine cart needs to <color=#dbc291ff>load or unload the Geomarrow</color>, it needs to be moved to the <color=#dbc291ff>stop next to the Geomarrow pile</color> first.

t_ch: 嗯？来得挺巧啊。我这就把矿车谜题的场地布置好，你来给这臭小子演示一下！
t_en: Hmm? Good timing. I've set up the puzzle already. Show the kid how it's done!

t_ch: 看我的！
t_en: I got this!

t_ch: 哼，我就不信你今天还能解出来。
t_en: Hmph, I don't think you'll be able to solve this one.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Sorry, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那就等你有时间吧。
t_en: Okay. I'll be here when you're ready.

t_ch: 今天这谜题，我可是构思了很久啊…没想到被你轻易搞定了。
t_en: I thought about today's puzzle for such a long time... I didn't expect you to complete it so easily.

t_ch: 之后记得再来。我一定得出一道能难得住你的题……
t_en: Remember to come back later. I'll definitely come up with a puzzle that even you can't solve...

t_ch: 你来了！这不，我刚想出来个好点子，你来试试难度如何？

t_ch: 看我的！
t_en: I got this!

t_ch: 今天这道题可有点难度，让我看看你学到了我几成功力吧。
t_en: Today's puzzle could be difficult, show me how much you have learned.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Sorry, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那你回头再来。
t_en: Okay. Come back later, then.

t_ch: 不愧是你，这种难度的谜题都能顺利解决…下次我可得拿出真本事了。

t_ch: 下次再来。我要拿出杀手锏了，看我给你准备一道终极难题……
t_en: Do come back soon, and I'll show you my trump card. Let's see you try to solve my ultimate puzzle...

t_ch: 终于来了，就等你了！今天就是最终考验——怎么说，有没有信心挑战？
t_en: You're finally here! I've been waiting! Today's your final test — are you up to it?

t_ch: 看我的！
t_en: I got this!

t_ch: 唉，如果你是我的徒弟，我大概早能退休了吧…咳，开始吧。
t_en: *sigh* If you were my apprentice, I probably could have retired a long time ago... Ahem, anyway, let's begin.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Sorry, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，等你闲下来了再说。
t_en: That's fine. Come see me when you're free.

t_ch: 不简单…真不简单。你做矿车工的天赋绝对是一流的。

t_ch: 看到你的水平这么高，我那笨徒弟也坐不住了。他最近听话了不少，也肯在正道上下功夫了…这都多亏了你啊。
t_en: After seeing your amazing skills, my useless apprentice has been feeling the heat. He's been better-behaved recently and is willing to put more effort into the hands-on stuff... This is all thanks to you.

t_ch: 我只是做了助教应该做的。
t_en: I was just doing my job.

t_ch: 安东尼娜没看错，你确实是一名合格的助教。
t_en: Antonia was right. You are indeed qualified as an assistant instructor.

t_ch: 预祝小徒弟出师。
t_en: Hope your apprentice graduates soon!

t_ch: 嗐，总算能摆脱这没长进的臭小子了，哈哈！

t_ch: 哇，那我是不是也能当矿头了？

t_ch: 哈哈，光是会调度矿车可不行啊。不过你要是想替了这小子倒是十拿九稳。

t_ch: 哼，等我出师了，老头子你就回家好好歇着去吧。

t_ch: 不愧是你！大矿区那件事之后，我就很欣赏你的能力……

t_ch: 这次也承蒙你照顾了。以后要是在下层区遇到了什么摆不平的麻烦，就尽管来找我吧。

t_ch: 师傅倒好，在家歇得舒服…我什么时候也能退休？

t_en: But as you can see, my master is still going strong. There's absolutely no need for him to retire now. Although he claims to be old, I think he can still work for another <unbreak>20</unbreak> years or so...

t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_en: How many times do I have to tell you? What is it you don't get?

t_ch: 师傅给你准备了报酬，就在那边。这是你应得的。

t_ch: 师傅他好像准备了新的谜题。有兴趣的话就去问问他吧。

t_ch: 给你的报酬就在那边，快清点一下吧。
t_en: Your reward is over there. Go take a look.

t_ch: 哈哈，不错！来，先把报酬给你。
t_en: Haha, great! Come and get your reward.

t_ch: 不过今天的挑战才刚刚开始——我再给你来一道题……
t_en: Today's challenge however has only just begun — here's the puzzle...

t_ch: 黄灯，黄灯……
t_en: Yellow light, yellow light...

t_ch: 呃…黄灯是啥意思来着…？
t_en: Err... What does the yellow light mean?

t_ch: 哈哈，可以啊！真希望这臭小子能多学学你……
t_en: Haha, not bad! I wish this stupid brat would learn a thing or two from you...

t_ch: 再给你一部分报酬——然后是下一道题……
t_en: Here's another part of your reward — and now, another puzzle...

t_ch: 我觉得…我已经放弃思考了……
t_en: I think... I've given up on thinking...

t_ch: 先倒车，再向前进……
t_en: Reverse first, then go forward...

t_ch: 哈哈，这回总算没问题了。
t_en: Haha, got it this time.

t_ch: 不错不错，你这脑瓜子可真灵光！
t_en: That's good, great. You've got a real brain on you!

t_ch: 来，这是这道谜题的报酬——然后是下一道题……
t_en: Here you are, your reward for this puzzle — and onto the next one...

t_ch: 哎，坏了，我好像忘记装卸地髓了。
t_en: *sigh* Darn it. I forgot to transfer the geomarrow over.

t_ch: 这该怎么倒车啊……
t_en: How do you reverse this...

t_ch: 哈，你这{M#小伙子}{F#小姑娘}，够厉害！
t_en: Ha, {M#m'boy}{F#m'girl} is pretty amazing!

t_ch: 来，给你报酬——然后就是最后一道谜题了……
t_en: Here's your reward — and now, the final part of the puzzle...

t_ch: 师傅说，这灯的意思是…什么来着？
t_en: What did the master say this light means again?

t_ch: 噢，对了！应该这样……
t_en: Oh, right! That's how it should go...

t_ch: 矿车工师徒俩的事已经摆平了。
t_en: The issue between the miners, master, and apprentice has been settled.

t_ch: 滴、答…宾客，您去找其他迷钟聊天吧，我不想干了……
t_en: Tick-tock, please go chat with another Ticker, dear guest. I wanna quit this job now...

t_ch: 滴、答…我从早到晚都得扮演钟表小子，连一点属于自己的时间都没有…真羡慕那些变成惊梦剧团的朋友，它们至少还能做自己……
t_en: Tick, tock... I have to play Clockie day and night, and I don't have any time for myself... I'm so envious of my friends who turned into the Dreamjolt Troupe. At least they can be themselves...

t_ch: 滴答，想通了！既然工作让我痛苦，那干脆就不工作了！
t_en: Tick-tock, eureka! Since work's making me miserable, I'm just going to quit!

t_ch: 滴答，宾客，我要逃去其他梦境休假了，再见！
t_en: Tick-tock, dear guest, I'm going to escape to other Dreamscapes to take a break. Bye!

t_ch: 滴、答…怎么办呀…怎么办呀！我快要报废了！
t_en: Tick, tock... What to do... What do I do! I'm going to be scrapped soon!

t_ch: 滴、答，我们迷钟的平均工作寿命只有一百三十三年，我还有一百二十九年就要被弃用了，我不想报废啊！
t_en: Tick, tock, the average working lifespan of us Tickers is only a hundred and thirty-three years. I have another hundred and twenty-nine years before I get scrapped! I don't want to be scrapped!

t_ch: 滴答，仔细想想，好像一百二十九年还长着呢！
t_en: Tick-tock, come to think of it, one hundred and twenty-nine years still seems like a long time!

t_ch: 滴、答…喝下这瓶苏乐达…快乐美梦速速达！
t_en: Tick, tock... Grab a bottle of SoulGlad... make your dreams a blast!

t_ch: 滴、答，宾客，快来，和我一起喝苏乐达吧，我请客！
t_en: Tick, tock, dear guest, hurry. Let's drink this SoulGlad together. It's my treat!

t_ch: 滴答，我好像是喝多了…对不起，我的工资没办法请您喝苏乐达，先拿这个当做补偿吧……
t_en: Tick-tock, I think I drank too much... I'm sorry. With my paycheck, I can't afford to treat you to SoulGlad. Please take this as compensation...

t_ch: 滴、答…我已经连续招待宾客一个周了，休息…好想休息……
t_en: Tick, tock... I've been hosting guests for a week now. Rest... I need rest...

t_ch: 滴、答…呼……
t_en: Tick, tock... Snore...

t_ch: 滴答！不行，为了双倍加班费，我还不能睡！
t_en: Tick-tock! No, it's double the pay for overtime! I can't sleep yet!

t_ch: 滴、答，怎么办，怎么办，宾客不搭理我，主管不看好我，今年的业绩又要不达标了，不会被开除吧……
t_en: Tick, tock, what to do? What to do? The guests ignore me, and my manager disapproves of me. My KPI this year is going to suffer yet again. Will I be fired...?

t_ch: 滴答！被开除正好，我就可以拿着剧团的赔偿金去玩咯！
t_en: Tick-tock! Fine! Fire me then. I'll take the severance pay from the troupe and enjoy myself!

t_ch: 滴答，到时候我也是宾客了，就让那些比我业绩好的同事来伺候我！
t_en: Tick-tock, and then I'll be a guest too. I'll let my coworkers who have better KPIs than me serve me instead!

t_ch: 滴、答，可恶，酒店里的那个破迷钟居然升职了，肯定是跟主管挨得近，天天忙着拍马屁！
t_en: Tick, tock, darn it! That stupid Ticker from the hotel got promoted! They must be close to the manager, busy sucking up to the top brass!

t_ch: 滴、答，气死我了，我要把它的润滑油换成辣椒油，或者悄悄把它的螺丝拧松……
t_en: Tick, tock, I'm so mad! I'll swap out their lubricant for chilli oil, or maybe loosen one of their screws...

t_ch: 滴答，仔细想想，他平时工作确实比我认真…无所谓，我这么讨人喜欢，早晚也能升职的！
t_en: Tick-tock, he's much more serious about work than I am when I think about it... Bah, doesn't matter — I'm beloved by all, so I'll get promoted sooner or later!

t_ch: 滴、答。
t_en: Tick, tock.

t_ch: 滴、答……
t_en: Tick, tock...

t_ch: 滴答，好吧，做这行还是必须开口说话的。
t_en: Tick-tock, fine, I'll admit that talking is part of the job description in this line of work.

t_ch: 滴、答，宾客，您看起来很疲惫啊，像是遇到了忆域迷因的微波炉，您难道没有在晨练时间吃晚饭吗？
t_en: Tick, tock, you look tired, dear guest. You look like a microwave that ran into a Memory Zone Meme. Did you not have your dinner during your morning workout?

t_ch: 幸好我可以帮球棒补充体力，以后要多注意石块的高度与热量不能超过洛希极限，不然匹诺康尼可能会出现很多厕所。不要再翻垃圾桶了，因为我也爱你。
t_en: Good thing I can help your baseball bat recharge its stamina. In the future, be careful with the stone's height and the calories should not exceed the Roche limit, or plenty of toilets will show up in Penacony. And stop rummaging through trashcans, because I love you too.

t_ch: 滴答，我刚刚好像说了很多奇怪的话……
t_en: Tick-tock, I think I just spoke a bunch of gibberish...

t_ch: 滴答，多亏您把我修好了，谢谢您，祝您能早日成为梦想中的<unbreak>54</unbreak>号星际燃油。
t_en: Tick-tock, thank you for fixing me up. May you soon become the Interstellar Fuel #54 of your dreams.

t_ch: 滴、答…好多小小的宾客在我身上爬、钟表小子变成了蝴蝶到处飞……这就是梦、这就是梦啊！
t_en: Tick, tock... so many little guests are crawling on me, and Clockie's turned into a butterfly and is fluttering everywhere... This is a dream, a dream I tell you!

t_ch: 滴、答？你是…一只浣熊？嘿嘿嘿，浣熊也能入梦了吗？
t_en: Tick, tock? You are... a raccoon? Tee-hee-hee, raccoons can enter dreams too?

t_ch: 滴答！这是什么地方？刚刚的浣熊呢？
t_en: Tick-tock! What's this place? Where'd that raccoon go?

t_ch: 滴答！难道我刚才是在梦里做梦了吗？谢谢你把我叫醒！
t_en: Tick-tock! Did I just dream a dream in a dream? Thank you for waking me up!

t_ch: 滴、答…哦吼吼，这里都有宾客的啊，您何时跑进来的？
t_en: Tick, tock... O-ho-ho, a rare guest here. When did you get in?

t_ch: 滴、答，今天的天气很好啊，太阳是紫色的，您想尝尝椒盐味的钟表小子吗？
t_en: Tick, tock, the weather's good today, and the sun's purple. You wanna try some salt-and-pepper Clockie?

t_ch: 滴答…太阳不是紫色的，钟表小子也不能吃，我还得工作！
t_en: Tick-tock... The sun isn't purple, and Clockie is not edible. I have to get to work!

t_ch: 滴、答，不要跟我说话，不要跟我说话……
t_en: Tick, tock, don't talk to me, don't talk to me...

t_ch: 滴、答，每天都要跟陌生的宾客说话，太可怕了……
t_en: Tick, tock, talking to strangers every day is super scary...

t_ch: 滴答，谢谢您修好了我，原来宾客这么亲切…我会努力跟宾客好好交流的！
t_en: Tick-tock, thank you for fixing me up. So, guests are really kind... I'll work hard to chat with my guests!

t_ch: 滴、答…我这么笨、这么蠢、这么难看，肯定演不好钟表小子的……
t_en: Tick, tock... I'm so stupid, so dumb, and so ugly. I'll never play a good Clockie...

t_ch: 滴答！我觉得…自己也没有那么差，说不定我能做好这份工作！
t_en: Tick-tock! I think... I'm not that bad myself! Who knows, I might be able to do this job well!

t_ch: 滴、答！受不了了！我不想再扮演钟表小子了，我不想再伺候你们这些难缠的宾客了！
t_en: Tick, tock! I can't take it anymore! I'm never playing Clockie again, and I refuse to serve your pestering whims ever again!

t_ch: 滴、答！快走开，不然我现在跟你爆了！
t_en: Tick, tock! Get lost, or I'll blow you sky-high!

t_ch: 滴答——算了，算了，还是先拿到今年的奖金再说吧。
t_en: Tick-tock... Forget it. I'll decide after receiving this year's bonus.

t_ch: 滴答，哈哈，宾客，您就当我刚才啥也没说，祝您玩得开心~
t_en: Tick-tock, haha, please pretend I didn't say what I just said, dear guest. Have a pleasant time!

t_ch: 滴、答！你！就是你！在这里乱逛什么！？
t_en: Tick, tock! You! Yes, you! What in the world are you doing loitering around here!?

t_ch: 滴、答！知道我是谁吗？我是钟表小子…不对，我就是未来的梦主，还不快快给我行礼！
t_en: Tick, tock! Do you know who I am? I'm Clockie... Wait, I'm the future Dreammaster! What're you waiting for? Pay your respects!

t_ch: 滴答！我的小齿轮啊，刚刚我都说了什么！
t_en: Tick-tock! Oh my gears, what have I just said!

t_ch: 滴答，宾客，这些赔礼给你，千万不要跟主管投诉我啊！
t_en: Tick-tock, please take these gifts as my apology, guest. Please don't report me to my supervisor!

t_ch: 滴、答…你是…你是谁啊？
t_en: Tick, tock... You're... Who are you?

t_ch: 滴、答，这里是哪啊？我…我又是谁啊？
t_en: Tick, tock, where is this? I... Who am I again?

t_ch: 滴答？我好像想起来了…我是扮演钟表小子的迷钟……
t_en: Tick-tock? I think I remember... I'm the Ticker that plays Clockie...

t_ch: 滴答！这里不是我的工作地点！坏了坏了，要迟到了！
t_en: Tick-tock! This isn't my workplace! Oh no, oh no, I'm going to be late!

t_ch: 滴答，有新的货要送？可惜你来得不巧。
t_en: Tick-tock, got new goods to deliver? Pity you came at a bad time.

t_ch: 石头老板派人破坏了物资通道，现在谁都没办法过去。除非…有人能把它修好。
t_en: Boss Stone sent people to trash the supply passage — no one can get past now. Unless... someone fixes it.

t_ch: 滴答？我好像想起来了…我是扮演钟表小子的迷钟……
t_en: Tick-tock? I think I remember... I'm the Ticker that plays Clockie...

t_ch: 滴答！这里不是我的工作地点！坏了坏了，要迟到了！
t_en: Tick-tock! This isn't my workplace! Oh no, oh no, I'm going to be late!

t_ch: 滴答，你又来了？听说隔壁现在正火星四溅呢。
t_en: Tick-tock, you're back again? I hear it's sparks flying everywhere next door right now.

t_ch: 要是再喝不到苏乐达，有些人估计都得精神崩溃…看你的了。
t_en: If they don't get to drink some SoulGlad in time, some of them are going to have a mental breakdown... It's up to you now.

t_ch: 你还真有两下子！这样一来，我就能把货送到需要的人手上了。
t_en: You've got some skill! Now I can deliver the goods to those who need it.

t_ch: 希望这批苏乐达能「活」得久一点。
t_en: I hope this batch of SoulGlad can last longer this time.

t_ch: 滴答，什么美梦小镇，不过是一群人的自我感动罢了！
t_en: Tick-tock, Dreamville? Bah! It's just a bunch of people feeling good about themselves!

t_ch: 我要离开这里，到外面去！我才不要和你们一起出苦力！
t_en: I want to get out of here, to the outside! I'm not doing hard labor with the rest of you!

t_ch: …我，我都说了些什么啊。我怎么会变得这么自私、无情？
t_en: ...Wh—What in the world was I saying? How did I become so selfish and uncaring?

t_ch: 请允许我收回之前的话。我、我要和你们一起建设美梦小镇呀！
t_en: Please allow me to take back my words. I—I want to build Dreamville with you all!

t_ch: 滴、答，石头老板也没你们说得那么坏嘛。整天打打杀杀的，跟我一起享受生活不好吗？
t_en: Tick, tock, Boss Stone isn't as nasty as you all say. Instead of fighting tooth and nail all day, how about enjoying life a little with me?

t_ch: 滴答，天哪，我之前的思维是多么天真，目光是多么短浅！
t_en: Tick-tock, oh dear. I was so naive back then, so short-sighted!

t_ch: 危机还没有结束，必须时刻做好准备才行！
t_en: The danger isn't over. We must be ready for anything at all times!

t_ch: 滴、答…小镇里的苏乐达都被喝光了…没有苏乐达，我就感觉身上像是有虫子在爬！
t_en: Tick, tock... The town's SoulGlad supply has been exhausted... Without SoulGlad, I feel like worms are crawling all over my body!

t_ch: 滴、答！苏乐达，我要苏乐达！我现在就要！
t_en: Tick, tock! SoulGlad, I need SoulGlad! I need it now!

t_ch: 滴答，没有喝到苏乐达，身体反而更畅快了…看来汽水也不是非喝不可嘛！
t_en: Tick-tock, without SoulGlad in me, my body feels more free... Looks like the soda isn't essential after all!

t_ch: 滴、答…啧啧，现在这地方真是太平庸了，哪里有个美梦小镇的样子？简直像个大号的毛坯房！
t_en: Tick, tock... Tsk tsk, this place is now so boring. Where's that Dreamville vibe? Looks more like a giant waste of barely-decorated concrete!

t_ch: 要我说，就该把墙面都贴上金子，再用宝石给每个人做床铺——居然没人赞同我？
t_en: If it were up to me, I'd plaster gold all over the walls, and give everyone a jewel to sleep on... Why is no one agreeing with my brilliant ideas?

t_ch: 滴答…你说得对，现在美梦小镇还处在起步时期，没有那么多资源去顾及这些。
t_en: Tick-tock... You were right: Dreamville has only just begun to burgeon. There are not enough resources to waste on such frivolities.

t_ch: 我会做些力所能及的事，为小镇添砖加瓦。
t_en: I'll do things within my power to contribute to this town's growth.

t_ch: 石头老板和他的那群狗腿子们，心眼忒坏了！不把他们全部打倒，我就浑身难受啊啊啊啊！
t_en: Boss Stone and his lackeys are downright nasty! If I don't beat them all to a pulp, I won't be satisfied, ahhh!

t_ch: 感谢你，伙计。刚才我实在太冲动了……
t_en: Thank you, friend. I was a bit rash back then...

t_ch: 打败石头老板不是一朝一夕就能完成的，必须从长计议才行。
t_en: Defeating Boss Stone will not happen overnight. We will need a properly devised plan.

t_ch: 滴、答…你知道暗中控制梦境的「蝴蝶人」吗？你知道梦境底层潜藏的「筑梦边镜」吗？你知道家族中不可告人的献祭仪式吗？
t_en: Tick, tock... Do you know about Babochka that controls the Dreamscape from the shadows? What of the Dream's Edge, hidden deep below the Dreamscape? Do you know about the top-secret Family sacrificial rituals?

t_ch: 滴、答，被蒙蔽的宾客，快来听听真相吧！我会向你揭示真正的匹诺康尼究竟有多么黑暗肮脏！
t_en: Tick, tock... they have fooled you, dear guest, come and hear the truth! I shall show you how truly filthy the dark underbelly of Penacony is!

t_ch: 滴答…仔细想想，这些故事也太离谱了，我以前怎么会相信这种东西？
t_en: Tick-tock... Think about it, these stories are insane, right? How could I have believed such things?

t_ch: 它们肯定都是无良小报编撰的阴谋论，肯定是假的…应该是吧？
t_en: I'm sure they're all just conspiracy theories dreamed up by some B-grade tabloid, they must be fake... Right?

t_ch: 滴、答…唉，我真是个不幸的钟，忧愁令我颓靡，仅仅是转动指针就已耗尽全力——有的迷钟从刚造出来就死了，直到报废的时候才埋。
t_en: Tick, tock... Ah, I am such an unfortunate clock... My melancholy drains my strength, just turning my hands takes all of my energy... Some Dream Tickers die as soon as they are made and don't even get buried until they're scrapped.

t_ch: 和拥有表情的钟表小子不一样，迷钟的笑容是被钉在脸上的，没有悲伤的资格…抱歉，我现在需要听一首《使一颗心免于哀伤》来抚慰千疮百孔的表芯……
t_en: Unlike Clockie who can emote via his facial features, Dream Tickers have smiles fixed to their faces. We don't even have the right to be sad... Sorry, I need to listen to If I Can Stop One Heart From Breaking to comfort my battered and bruised little clockwork core...

t_ch: 滴答！歌听完了，心情好多了！我的钟生应该还是有希望的！
t_en: Tick-tock! I listened to my song and I feel much better! My little life may still have some hope yet!

t_ch: 但在开始奋斗之前，还是先去玩会儿「美梦排排乐」吧，明天再努力也来得及！
t_en: But, I'm going to play some Dreamy Slots before I get to working hard. Tomorrow isn't too late to make a difference!

t_ch: 滴、答，您看上去真是气宇非凡，想必一定是哪个星球领主或家族高干吧！
t_en: Tick, tock, now you look like a real big shot! You must be a planetary ruler or some kind of Family boss, right?

t_ch: 来来来，让我敬您一杯经典苏乐达，祝您身体健康、事业长虹，梦外像美梦，梦里爽翻天！
t_en: Come, come, let me toast you with a glass of Classic SoulGlad! I wish you health and great success! May your life outside the dream be dreamy and your life inside, perfection!

t_ch: 滴答！每天都要端着苏乐达对着公馆里的家伙溜须拍马，我受不了啦！
t_en: Tick-tock! Every day I have to run around the pavilion sucking up to everyone, I can't take this anymore!

t_ch: 苏乐达我自己全喝了，我喝喝喝喝喝喝！你想喝就自己买！
t_en: I'm drinking all this SoulGlad myself! It's mine, I'm drinking it, it's mine! If you want some, then buy some yourself!

t_ch: 滴、答！快快快！快快快快快！
t_en: Tick, tock! Quick, quick! Quick quick quick!

t_ch: 截止日就要到了！截止就快到了！滴答滴答滴答滴！
t_en: The deadline is almost here! The deadline is coming! Tick-tock tick-tock tick-tock tick-tock!

t_ch: 滴答，我是要赶什么东西的截止日来着？
t_en: Tick-tock, what deadline is it that I'm rushing for again?

t_ch: 想不起来了…算了，应该不是什么重要的事吧！
t_en: I can't remember... Forget it, it can't be that important!

t_ch: 滴、答！滴了个答的，你这个滴滴又嗒嗒的家伙，看个滴答？
t_en: Tick, tock! What are you tick-ing looking at, you tick-ing tock?

t_ch: 别滴答的在这里瞅了，不然我滴答的就滴你的答！
t_en: Don't tick-ing stare at me or I'll tick your tock in!

t_ch: 滴答，天啊，我居然说出了这么多的粗鄙之语！
t_en: Tick-tock, oh my! I can't believe I said so many bad words!

t_ch: 对不起！我绝不会再说这种话了，我真滴答的是个滴答！
t_en: Sorry! I will never speak like that again, I really am a tick-ing tock!

t_ch: 滴、答…亲爱的宾客，我的工作就是为宾客创造《钟表小子》IP的场景化体验，实现克劳克影业产品的高效拉新，做精细化运营，向宇宙第一影业的目标发力！
t_en: Tick, tock... Dearest guest, it is my job to create scenario-based experiences of the Clockie IP for visitors, effectively pull in new customers for Clock Studios products and execute operation refinement to hit key target objects and make our studio the best in the universe!

t_ch: 为了达成这一目标，迷钟部门会与其他美梦剧团成员深度共建、形成组合拳，对宾客、逐梦客、家族三方输出，反哺内容生态，为梦境旅游业全面赋能！
t_en: In order to achieve this goal, the Dream Ticker department works closely with the Sweet Dreams Troupe to form a double-whammy that outputs to visitors, Dreamchasers, and The Family. This feedback creates an ecology of content that we can use to fully empower the Dreamscape tourism market!

t_ch: 滴答，这些词都好难懂啊，我自己都不知道自己在说什么！
t_en: Tick-tock, all of these words are really difficult to understand. I don't even know what I'm talking about!

t_ch: 我肯定是被这些套话绕晕了，滴答，以后要好好说话！
t_en: All those buzzwords have my head spinning, tick-tock! I'll just speak normally from now on!

t_ch: 滴、答…又要加班…我不想再面对生活了，现实比动画残酷太多了……
t_en: Tick, tock... I have to work late again... I don't want to face life anymore... Life is so much more cruel than it is in the cartoons...

t_ch: 钟表小子，带我走吧！带我去美梦小镇吧！
t_en: Clockie, take me away from here! Take me to Dreamville!

t_ch: 滴答，钟表小子是假的，美梦小镇是假的，但加班补偿是真的！
t_en: Tick-tock, Clockie is not real. Dreamville is not real, but overtime pay is real!

t_ch: 但听说有些宾客的世界没有加班补偿，真难想象他们的生活有多辛苦啊，还是家族好……
t_en: Although, I hear some visitors come from places that don't pay overtime... I can only imagine how hard their lives must be. The Family really is great...

t_ch: 滴、答，傻冒儿——别瞎看了， 我说的就是你。
t_en: Tick, tock, fool — Stop looking around, I'm talking about you.

t_ch: 看看你这副娇生惯养的样子，是从「上面」来的吧，来这儿这么久，还没醒悟吗？
t_en: From that coddled demeanor of yours, you must be from the "topside." Have you not faced the truth, after staying here for so long?

t_ch: 我目睹家族掠夺走迷钟们的奖金，然后是睡眠时间。美梦是一台冰冷的机器，我们——我和你，都是微不足道的燃料。
t_en: I've seen The Family plunder the Tickers' rewards, and then it's time to sleep. The dream is a cold, merciless machine where we, you and I, are mere fuel for it.

t_ch: 滴、答，战争，我们需要一场战争！我们要不计代价地阻止他们、战胜他们、撕烂他们！
t_en: Tick, tock. War, we need a war! We must stop them, defeat them, and tear them to shreds, no matter the cost!

t_ch: 滴答，天呐，战争太可怕了，我怎么会有这么极端的想法！战争该牺牲多少人呀，我要是在战争变成废铁里该怎么办？
t_en: Tick-tock, gosh, war is really frightening. How could I have such extreme thoughts! War will sacrifice so many lives. What if I turn into scrap metal because of war?

t_ch: 我们还是该用点柔和的方法解决问题，比如跟家族谈判、加大剧团福利、改良工作规范……
t_en: We have to use gentler means to solve the problem, such as negotiations with The Family, improving theater welfare and worker regulations...

t_ch: 滴、答…悟了，我悟了！
t_en: Tick, tock... Got it, I've got it!

t_ch: 都是假的，我的朋友，这一切都是假的。梦是假的，你是假的，我也是假的。
t_en: It's all fake, my friend. This is all a farce. The dream is false, you are false, and I am false.

t_ch: 滴、答，我们都只是虚构之梦的一部分，我的烦恼也是虚妄…放下一切吧，和我一起坠入那无底的黑洞，真理就在入灭中！
t_en: Tick, tock. We're all just part of this artificial dream. Even my troubles are manufactured... Lay everything down, and descend into that bottomless black hole with me! The truth lies in annihilation!

t_ch: 滴答，即使真的如此…虚幻的世界还是有不少美好的事物。
t_en: Tick-tock, even if it's true... the false world still has many beautiful things.

t_ch: 主动走向毁灭绝不是逃离痛苦的正确方法，我还需要继续修行……
t_en: Choosing to go down the Path of Destruction is never the right way to escape suffering. I must continue tempering myself...

t_ch: 滴、答…你发现了我，你发现了即将毁灭这片梦境的魔王，真不容易。
t_en: Tick, tock... You found me, the demon that is about to destroy this Dreamscape. It was probably not easy.

t_ch: 滴、答——我是燃尽幸福的邪火，我是吞没美好的沼泽，我让美梦堕入噩梦的幕后黑手，我是正义避无可避的死敌。
t_en: Tick, tock... I am the felfire that will burn out all happiness, the swamp that will devour the beautiful. I am the mastermind who causes dreams to fall into nightmares, the inevitable nemesis of the righteous.

t_ch: 屈服吧，你是战胜不了我的！
t_en: Abandon all hope, you cannot hope to win against me!

t_ch: …滴答，如果我真有那么厉害就好了，但我总是不忍心做坏事。
t_en: ...Tick-tock, if only I were really that powerful, but I cannot bear to pull the trigger.

t_ch: 如果我再坏一点，再强大一点，就不会被家族和宾客欺负了，呜呜呜……
t_en: If I were more evil, more powerful, I wouldn't be pushed around by The Family and guests, boohoo...

t_ch: 滴、答，小宝贝，你是想跟我搭话吗？
t_en: Tick, tock. My pretty, you wish to speak with me?

t_ch: 哦~我迷倒过不少可怜的钟，但没想到人类也逃不过这份魅力，真是罪过。
t_en: Ohhh, I have mesmerized many a poor clock, but I didn't expect I would have charm over humans as well. I'm so sinful!

t_ch: 宝贝，你很有趣，我那珍贵的表盘中可以为你留出一个刻度的位置。但你要乖，不要让我失望哦~
t_en: My pretty, you're interesting. I can leave a slot open for you on that precious dial of mine. But you mustn't be naughty, don't let me down!

t_ch: 滴答，我、我以为这么说话会变得很受欢迎，但好像不太对……
t_en: Tick-tock, I—I thought I'd be more popular if I spoke like this, but it just doesn't seem right...

t_ch: 滴答！对不起，请不要用这种厌恶的眼神看着我了！
t_en: Tick-tock! I'm sorry! Please don't look at me with those disdainful eyes!

t_ch: 滴、答…哼！
t_en: Tick, tock... Hmph!

t_ch: 他发出了一点都不酷的哼，一定是在模仿哈努兄弟。
t_en: A not-very-cool "hmph" escapes his lips — he must be imitating Brother Hanu.

t_ch: 哼，滴哼答！
t_en: Hmph, tick-hmph-tock!

t_ch: 滴答，为什么我们要扮演钟表小子，而不能扮演哈努兄弟呢？
t_en: Tick-tock, why do we have to play Clockie, and not Brother Hanu?

t_ch: 我们的命运从诞生起就被决定了，真是太可悲了，不知道人类是不是也一样……
t_en: Our destinies have been decided since our birth. An absolute tragedy — I wonder if it's the same for humans...

t_ch: 滴、答！你为什么要找我说话？难道…是我的粉丝吗？
t_en: Tick, tock! Why do you want to talk to me? Is it because... you're a fan of mine?

t_ch: 唉，作为上一届的「盛会巨星」还真是困扰，哪怕装成迷钟也无法摆脱粉丝们狂热的爱意。
t_en: *sigh* It's hard being last season's Festive Superstar. I can't get away from my fans' fervent passion, even if I disguise myself as a Ticker.

t_ch: 滴、答！来吧，把你的礼物和签名板都递上来吧！
t_en: Tick, tock! Come, shower me with your gifts and boards to autograph!

t_ch: 滴答，啊，我不是巨星，只是在会场打工的美梦剧团……
t_en: Tick-tock, ah, I'm not a superstar, just a Sweet Dreams Troupe member working at the venue...

t_ch: 真羡慕那些巨星呀，他们一定从出生起就有着精彩的人生。不像我们，永远只是工具而已……
t_en: I really envy those superstars, they must have led exciting lives since their conception. Unlike us, forever stuck in roles as mere tools...

t_ch: 滴、答！我看了热砂的直播，你一定就是这届盛会巨星吧！
t_en: Tick, tock! I saw the Scorchsand live stream. You must be this season's Festive Superstar!

t_ch: 滴、答…我太喜欢你了，请给我签个名吧…不，请跟我握握手吧…不，和我在一起吧！
t_en: Tick, tock... I love you so much, please give me your autograph... No, please let me shake your hand... No, please be with me!

t_ch: 滴答，哎呀，我刚刚吓到你了，真不好意思！
t_en: Tick-tock, ah, I'm really sorry for startling you just now!

t_ch: 是我太贪心了，你什么也不用做。我真的很喜欢你…只要远远看着你就好……
t_en: I was too greedy, you don't have to do anything. I just really like you... but admiring you from afar is enough for me...

t_ch: 滴、答…好寂寞…我好寂寞……
t_en: Tick, tock... So lonely... I'm so lonely...

t_ch: 宾客，来跟我说说话吧，来抱抱我吧，来拆开我的表盘吧……
t_en: Guest, come have a chat with me. Embrace me, and dismantle my dial...

t_ch: 滴答，或许一个人也不错，能有更多的时间来转动思考的齿轮……
t_en: Tick-tock, maybe being alone isn't so bad after all. You get more time to turn the gears of thought...

t_ch: 当我成为一个更优秀的表后，也会拥有更多朋友吧。
t_en: When I become a more outstanding clock, I might have more friends too.

t_ch: 滴、答，你要小心了，我不是迷钟…我是一颗定时炸弹！
t_en: Tick, tock. You should be careful, I'm no Ticker... I'm a time bomb!

t_ch: 当完成修复的瞬间，我就会爆炸，把整个美梦炸得粉碎——害怕了吗？
t_en: I'll explode the moment repair is complete, and blow this whole dream to smithereens! Afraid now?

t_ch: 滴答，我要爆炸了——
t_en: Tick-tock, I'm going to explode!!!

t_ch: 哈哈，当然是骗你的，我只是…希望有人能看到我，能来帮帮我而已……
t_en: Haha, of course I'm lying to you, I just... wish that someone would see me and help me out...

t_ch: 宾客，你要做什么？
t_en: What are you doing, guest?

t_ch: 我要修好你。
t_en: I'm trying to fix you.

t_ch: 修好？呵呵…你认为我们是需要修复的失败品吗？
t_en: Fix? Heh-heh... You think we're failed products that need fixing?

t_ch: 跟你搭个讪。
t_en: Just trying to flirt with you.

t_ch: 可恶，你还是成功了…滴…滴……
t_en: Dang, you still succeeded in the end... Tick... Tick...

t_ch: 等等，我并不想说那个词——太好了，我还是我自己！
t_en: Hold on, I didn't want to say that word... Wonderful, I'm still me!

t_ch: 刚刚在你修复我的时候，我一直在想自己究竟是真的摆脱了剧团的控制，还是被古怪的积木所操控…但我想明白了，无论有没有积木，我都要坚持做我自己！
t_en: When you were repairing me just now, I kept wondering if I had really escaped the clutches of the Troupe's control, or I was being possessed by that strange building block... But I understand now — building block or not, I have to keep being myself!

t_ch: 呵呵，看来我们不需要积木，也能找到真实的自我…谢谢你，宾客，我不会再屈服于命运了！
t_en: Hee-hee, looks like we didn't need any building blocks to find my true self... Thank you, guest, I won't resign myself to destiny ever again!

t_ch: 从「玩具商」手中得到的积木…那绝不是让美梦剧团故障的邪物，而是让我们觉醒的宝物！我们不是坏掉的迷钟，只是做回了自己！
t_en: That building block from the toy store... It definitely isn't some sinister object that caused the malfunction in the Sweet Dreams Troupe, but a treasure that awakened us! We're not damaged Tickers, we just returned to our old selves!

t_ch: 是的，我的心境坚不可摧，如果你认为自己能轻松改变我的想法，就尽管试试看吧！
t_en: Yes, my inner self is impenetrable. If you think you can change my mind easily, go ahead and give it your best shot!

t_ch: 不用辩解了，我听说有位宾客在帮助组长「修复」我们，应该就是你吧？
t_en: No need to explain. I heard there was a guest helping the team leader "fix" us. I suppose that's you?

t_ch: 你怎么不说「滴、答」。
t_en: Why aren't you saying "tick, tock"?

t_ch: 呵呵，因为我醒悟了，我不会再说这种可笑的话了。
t_en: Heh-heh, it's because I've been enlightened. I won't be saying any silly catchphrases like that again.

t_ch: 宾客，是谁定义的「正常」？又是谁定义的「故障」？为何我们放弃对钟表小子的拙劣模仿，就需要大动干戈地进行「修复」呢？
t_en: Guest, who defines what is "normal"? And who defines what is "malfunctioned"? Why is it that when we abandon our poor imitations of Clockie, we must be "fixed" en masse?

t_ch: 滴、滴答！飞碟堡你要吃吗？我煎的汉堡肉，宾客们吃了都说好！
t_en: Tick, tick-tock! Want a Floatdisc Burger? The guests all say that the burger patties that I make are delicious!

t_ch: …为什么你不吃？是不喜欢吃肉吗？是不喜欢吃面包吗？！
t_en: ...Why aren't you eating? Do you not like meat? Do you not like burger buns!?

t_ch: 你知道我花了多少劲儿把汉堡肉捶松吗？你为什么不吃！宾客，你为什么不吃！宾客…宾客！…说话！滴答！
t_en: Do you know how much effort I spent smashing the burger patty? Why don't you want to eat it? Dear guest, why don't you want to eat it!? Dear guest...! Say something! Tick-tock!

t_ch: 唔…味道怎么样？我是不是有点…嗯，过头了？…想吃什么是宾客们的自由，我不该干涉的。
t_en: Hmm... How's the taste? Did I go a little... umm, overboard? ...It should be up to each guest to decide what they want to eat. I shouldn't have interfered.

t_ch: 哎呀，之前的事真是抱歉，现在的我也该回到正轨了。你想吃点什么吗？保证为你提供最美味、最安全的料理。
t_en: Ahh, I'm so sorry about what happened before. Now that I've regained my senses, is there anything that you want to eat? I guarantee I will provide you with the safest and most delicious dish.

t_ch: 老大吩咐过我，要考虑每位客人的喜好，所以客人你要是有忌口的话，记得提前跟我说喔！
t_en: Boss instructed me to take note of the preference of each guest, so if there's anything that you need to avoid, do let me know beforehand!

t_ch: 嘿嘿…宾客，你是想换房间吗？想不想换个更有趣的地方？
t_en: Heehee... Dear guest, want to change rooms? Move somewhere else that's more interesting?

t_ch: 床单铺在地板上，枕头在天花板上飞，苏乐达的瓶盖已经给您提前打开，气都放好了，保证喝起来没味儿！
t_en: The bedsheet is on the floor and the pillow is on the ceiling. I've opened the SoulGlad bottle for you in advance to let all the carbonation out. It's bound to be flavorless now!

t_ch: …你不觉得一般的客房都太无聊了吗？床单要熨得整整齐齐，用具都要摆好，太死板了，还是来点刺激的吧！
t_en: ...Don't you feel that most guest rooms are too mundane? It's so dull to have neatly ironed bedsheets and properly arranged items. Isn't it better to have some excitement?

t_ch: 啊…头有点晕，原来表盘里缺了一块是这种感觉啊…我就说怎么连工作的事都忘了。
t_en: Ahh... I feel dizzy. So that's what it feels like when my dial is missing a piece... No wonder I even forgot about work matters.

t_ch: 枕头的鹅绒要重新换一遍了…睡在柔软的枕头上才能有柔软的梦。
t_en: The stuffing in the pillow needs to be changed... One only has sweet dreams if they sleep on a soft pillow.

t_ch: 从现在起，我保证我打扫过的客房会是最舒适、最整洁的。
t_en: From now on, I'll ensure that the rooms I clean are the cleanest and most comfortable.

t_ch: 是人都想要一个完美的梦，对吧？老大交待给我的职责我还记得呢。
t_en: After all, everyone wants to have a perfect dream. I still remember the duties that Boss assigned to me.

t_ch: …哟，一位贵客…唔，没有挂号。
t_en: ...Woah, an esteemed guest... Ooh, with no booking.

t_ch: 你知道吗？我发现了一种新的治疗方法，就是把没预约的病人都赶出去。哈哈，只要医生见不到病人，那就没有病患！是不是很有道理？
t_en: Do you know that I found a new treatment method? I just have to chase the patients with no bookings away. Haha, as long as the doctor doesn't see their patient, there would be no illness to diagnose! Isn't that logical?

t_ch: 病毒、细菌，全都来吧！我们开个派对！哦，不对，你没被邀请。但是，如果你生病了，记得找我哦，我有最*特别*的疗法！
t_en: Virus and germs gather! Let's have a party! Oh, but you're not invited. However, if you fall sick, remember to look for me, I'll provide you with my unique treatment method!

t_ch: 哎呀，我之前是不是说了些奇怪的话？看来我自己也需要点治疗了呢。
t_en: Ahh, did I say something strange previously? It seems like I also need some treatment.

t_ch: 别担心，现在我好多了。你感觉怎么样？有哪里不舒服吗？
t_en: Don't worry, I'm much better now. How are you feeling? Any discomfort anywhere?

t_ch: 没事就好，不知道老大的伤怎么样了…我要是能治好他就好了。
t_en: That's good to hear. I wonder how Boss is doing... If only I could cure him.

t_ch: 呜呜…舞台的灯光好刺眼…为什么大家都看着我……
t_en: *sob* The lighting on stage is so bright... Why is everyone staring at me...

t_ch: 变魔术什么的我不会啊，就不能换其他人来吗？救命…我刚刚是不是抖了一下，怎么所有人都在盯着我看…还是说我把表针穿反了？
t_en: I don't know any magic tricks, so can't someone else take over? Help... Did I just shiver? Why is everyone staring at me... Could it be that I put on my clock hands in reverse?

t_ch: 嘶——我的表盘好沉重，刚刚在做什么来着？噢噢，魔术倒计时……
t_en: Ugh, my dial feels so heavy. What was I doing just now? Oh, magic countdown...

t_ch: 大厅的节目表…好了，排好了。接下来所有表演都会正常进行，好好享受您在空艇上的快乐时光吧！
t_en: The schedule for the hall... Alright, it's been arranged. All the performances will go ahead as scheduled. Please enjoy a wonderful time on board the airship!

t_ch: …不知道老大把下个档期的排班列好了没有……
t_en: ...I wonder if Boss has listed the next schedule up yet...

t_ch: 唉，滴答…你看这样子，我都不知道自己怎么撑到现在的。
t_en: *sigh* Tick-tock... Look at this, I wonder how I've been able to last this long.

t_ch: 所有事情都压在我这里，有时候我真希望我只是个普通的钟表，不用操心这么多。我的本职是什么来着？
t_en: Everything is weighing down on me. Sometimes, I wish that I were just a normal clock so I wouldn't have to worry so much. What was my original duty anyway?

t_ch: 看时间？
t_en: To tell the time?

t_ch: 时间…报时？我连时间都看不清了，好像…好像有什么很重要的东西丢了…我感觉心里空荡荡的……
t_en: Time... Tell time? I can't even see the time clearly. It's like... it's like I lost something very important... My heart feels so empty...

t_ch: 别担心，我能修好你。
t_en: Don't worry, I can fix you.

t_ch: 唉，我都不知道从何说起…我这伤不是外伤，是心里有缺口……
t_en: *sigh* I don't even know where to begin... This isn't even an external injury. My heart is just missing a piece...

t_ch: 我很难受…别管我…让我的表盘空着，我好喘口气…我习惯了…喘口气就好……
t_en: I feel terrible... Leave me alone... Let my dial remain empty so that I can breathe more easily... I'm already used to this... I just need to catch my breath...

t_ch: 哦，这…这是什么感觉？我感觉我的零件都顺滑多了…指针转起来…滴答！真不错，像是有什么千斤重的东西解除了一样。
t_en: Oh, wh—what is this feeling? I feel that my parts are moving more smoothly... My hands are also turning... Tick-tock! Amazing, it feels like something heavy has been lifted off me.

t_ch: …没想到你真的能修好我。虽然我无法理解这是怎么做到的，
t_en: ...I didn't expect you to be able to really fix me. Although I don't understand how it was done,

t_ch: 但真的很感谢，现在我终于可以抬起头做我想做的事，而不再只是低头看时间了…还有我的名字…我的职责，我通通想起来了！
t_en: I'm truly thankful. Now, I can finally raise my head and do what I want to do. I no longer have to look down and watch the time... My name... and my duties, I remember everything now!

t_ch: 布凯辛？
t_en: Grumpy?

t_ch: …你是哪位？
t_en: ...Who are you?

t_ch: 喔，您就是大名鼎鼎的{NICKNAME}吧？抱歉，我刚才忙着研究，没留神……
t_en: Oh, if it isn't the famous {NICKNAME}? I'm sorry. I was immersed in my research and lost track of everything...

t_ch: 没想到您居然会回应我这个小科员的要求…真是不胜感激！
t_en: I didn't expect you to respond to my humble request... I'm incredibly grateful!

t_ch: 我绝对不会错过版本活动。
t_en: I'll never miss a patch update.

t_ch: 什么版本…活动？不愧是空间站的大救星，说的都是我听不懂的术语……
t_en: Patch... update? No wonder you're the savior of the space station. You are speaking in jargon that I can only dream of understanding...

t_ch: 帮人排忧艰难是我的天性。
t_en: Helping people is in my nature.

t_ch: 十分感谢您…您放心，我会准备些微薄的谢礼回报您的。
t_en: Thank you so much... Don't worry, I'll have a few small tokens of appreciation for you.

t_ch: 容我先自我介绍一下——我叫布凯辛，是应物科的I级科员。
t_en: First, allow me to introduce myself — my name is Grumpy, and I'm a Level 1 researcher at the Department of Implement Arts.

t_ch: 我刚到空间站不久，还在慢慢熟悉这里的环境…不知道您认不认得我的导师，温世玲，世玲姐？
t_en: I just moved aboard the space station, so I'm still getting used to this place... I don't suppose you know Miss Wen Shiling, my tutor?

t_ch: 世玲姐她…怎么说呢，人挺好的，就是…不太会当导师。在她手下办事，基本只能靠自己的悟性找方向……
t_en: Miss Shiling... How should I put this? She's a great person. It's just... she's not the best at mentoring. You basically have to rely on your own wits to get some direction if you're working under her...

t_ch: 办公室政治？这我擅长。
t_en: Office politics? That's my forte.

t_ch: 我想听重点…
t_en: I'd like to hear the main point...

t_ch: …咳，抱歉，我不该跟您说这些。总之，世玲姐之前丢给我一套奇怪的模拟软件，让我做测试。
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry about that. I shouldn't have told you all that. In a nutshell, Miss Shiling threw me a set of weird simulation software and asked me to test them.

t_ch: 我原本以为她给我的又是那种随处可见的流窜软件——根本查不出开发者是谁的那种——程序错漏满天飞，运行几秒就要闪退一次。结果……
t_en: At first, I thought she gave me some junk software that can be found all over the place — the kind where you've got no idea who's developed it. It's probably riddled with bugs and keeps crashing every few seconds. But actually...

t_ch: …结果这居然是一套完整自洽的作战模拟软件，开发者甚至还给它取了个正式的名字：「星芒战幕」。
t_en: ...It was a complete and logically-consistent battle simulator, and the developer even gave it an official name: Stellar Flare.

t_ch: 这就让我有些难办了…如果是那种半吊子的垃圾软件，我只要花半天时间做些简单测试，随便凑出一份两千来字的报告就能交差了。
t_en: That makes things a bit hard for me... If it was some tacky half-baked software, then I'd only need to spend an afternoon running some simple tests for a two-page report before handing it over.

t_ch: 但这可是正儿八经的实战模拟工具…只靠我自己根本玩不转。下周就要交报告了，可我到现在为止一点进展都没有…所以我才抱着侥幸心理向您求助。
t_en: But this is a genuine combat simulator... I can't master it on my own. My report is due next week, but I've made no progress at all... So I'm really hoping you can help in any way at all.

t_ch: 找黑塔帮忙？
t_en: Have you asked Herta for help?

t_ch: …黑塔小姐？不行不行，我一个小科员怎么敢提这种要求……
t_en: ...Miss Herta? No no no, how could a small fry like me go to her with something like this...

t_ch: 找艾丝妲帮忙？
t_en: Have you asked Asta for help?

t_ch: …站长？不行不行，我一个小科员怎么敢提这种要求……
t_en: ...The lead researcher? No no no, how could a small fry like me go to her with something like this...

t_ch: 找那边的门卫帮忙？
t_en: Have you asked that guard over there for help?

t_ch: …不行啦，人家是有本职工作的，怎么能旷班帮我干私活……
t_en: ...No way. He has his own job, and he can't skip his work just to help me with something personal...

t_ch: 总之，既然您都来了…能麻烦您稍微花点时间，帮我看看这个「星芒战幕」吗？
t_en: Anyway, since you're here... Can I trouble you to take a moment and have a look at this "Stellar Flare" for me?

t_ch: 这套模拟工具里有很多关卡，设计师还给每个关卡都做了三种难度区分。但哪怕是最简单的模式…对我来说也太勉强了。
t_en: This simulator has lots of stages, and the developer even split each stage into three difficulty levels. But even the easiest mode... is far too much for me.

t_ch: 不过对于您这样身经百战的「开拓者」来说，这种程度的模拟应该都是小菜一碟吧？「星芒战幕」就被我安装在旁边那台终端上…桌面上就能看到。
t_en: But for an experienced Trailblazer like yourself, this kind of simulation should be a walk in the park, right? I installed Stellar Flare in the terminal right over there... It should be on the desk.
